my_thoughts: 'I don't see why I have to do all the work here'—classic passive demand. They want therapy benefits without effort. But notice: they're WORKING to resist (arguing, defending, avoiding). Teacher's reframe: 'you ARE doing the work' points this out. Resistance takes energy. The question is whether they'll USE that energy for therapy or against it. Hypothesis: if they see that resistance = work, they might redirect the energy. Or they might double down. Single intervention, watching response.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student challenges: 'I don't see why I have to do all the work here'—defiant demand for teacher to do the work (passive_omnipotence: 'you do it for me'). Teacher reframes: 'you ARE doing the work' by showing that resistance itself is work. Uses none mechanism with confrontation: points out the energy they're expending to resist. Predicting go_along_compliantly because reframe might produce recognition that resistance = effort.
visible_response:
Thank you for being so collaborative.
Although your feet have brought you here, you are also letting me know you don’t want to depend on me.
